formula.tier2_ceiling: Forward EPS × High P/E × (1 − ½ MoS)
formula.eps_source: Analyst consensus 1-year forward estimate (or normalized mid-cycle EPS where noted)
notes: MoS = Margin of Safety — applied in full to T1 floor, half to T2 ceiling
T1 = deep-value entry zone (conservative multiple, full discount)
T2 = fair-value zone (full multiple range, half discount — guides tranche 2 sizing)
Band width signals valuation uncertainty: wide = high uncertainty, narrow = high confidence
Reassess MoS tiers when macro triggers fire — see reassessment_triggers section
v3: Added T3 Bear Floor (recession scenario floor below T1)
v3: Added Execution Confirmation Layer (RSI, MACD, SMA timing signals)

bear_discount_tiers.defensive_stable: 20% below T1 floor (MSFT, NEE, XLU, HESM, HPQ, AVGO, EWL)
bear_discount_tiers.growth_tech: 30% below T1 floor (NVDA, MRVL, GOOGL, CEG, TSM, SOXQ, SHEL)
bear_discount_tiers.cyclical_high: 35-40% below T1 floor (MU, WDC, LEU, LLY, NVO, EWY, FSLR, REMX, FRVO, BP)

execution_confirmation.description: Before deploying capital when price reaches T1 zone, check 2+ of 4 signals
signals: MACD daily converging — histogram bars shrinking or bullish cross
Price at/below 50-day SMA — institutional support alignment
Price below 9-day EMA — confirms drawdown, not a dip on uptrend
execution_confirmation.rule: 2+ signals = HIGH CONVICTION, scale up tranche; 0-1 signals = minimum tranche only

single_stock: Earnings miss or beat >15% vs consensus
Management guidance cut >10% on revenue or EPS
CEO/CFO departure or major insider selling
New SEC investigation or class action filed
sector_wide: New trade restriction or export control affecting sector
portfolio_wide_add_5pct_mos: Fed rate change ±50bps or more in single meeting
Annual review: every January and July

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.rationale: AI infrastructure monopoly; durable pricing power

MRVL.name: Marvell Technology
MRVL.rationale: Custom ASIC / AI networking; high growth, early cycle

AVGO.name: Broadcom
AVGO.rationale: AI custom silicon + software; mature, dividend payer

MU.name: Micron Technology
MU.rationale: Use mid-cycle normalized EPS — NOT peak. HBM adds premium but cycle risk is severe
MU.eps_override_note: Use analyst mid-cycle normalized EPS (~$50/share estimate), not peak forward EPS

WDC.name: Western Digital
WDC.rationale: NAND/HDD; more commodity than DRAM. Restructured — EV/EBITDA preferred

FSLR.name: First Solar
FSLR.rationale: Solar; cyclical + policy-sensitive — use mid-cycle EPS
FSLR.eps_note: Mid-cycle normalized EPS $18 (2026 consensus $17.41–$18.04; use normalized not peak)

LEU.name: Centrus Energy
LEU.rationale: Strategic monopoly premium: only US uranium enrichment at scale; wide range reflects policy/cycle uncertainty

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.rationale: Cloud + AI platform; highest quality in universe — minimal discount needed

GOOGL.name: Alphabet
GOOGL.rationale: Search + Cloud + AI; antitrust risk partially priced in MoS

HPQ.name: HP Inc
HPQ.rationale: PC / printer; mature, shrinking TAM, buyback story
HPQ.eps_note: Non-GAAP FY2026E EPS $3.00 (company guidance $2.90–$3.10 raised Q2 2026)

LLY.name: Eli Lilly
LLY.rationale: GLP-1 + oncology pipeline; high P/E revised 50→35× to reflect ATH-implied ceiling (~34.5×) and increased GLP-1 competition (NVO precedent)
LLY.eps_note: FY2026E consensus EPS ~$37 (range $34.79–$41.91); high_pe revised Jun 23 2026

NVO.name: Novo Nordisk
NVO.rationale: GLP-1 pioneer; re-rated down on LLY competition. Low end reflects pessimistic scenario, high end reflects durable franchise

BRK-B.name: Berkshire Hathaway B
BRK-B.rationale: Conglomerate quality + insurance float + equity portfolio; P/B cross-check recommended

FRVO.name: Fervo Energy
FRVO.rationale: Pre-revenue geothermal; EV/PPA contracted backlog per share ($26.77). Not P/E — no earnings yet

EWY.name: iShares MSCI South Korea ETF
EWY.rationale: Korea market weighted P/E; historically 9–13× with geopolitical discount

EWL.name: iShares MSCI Switzerland ETF
EWL.rationale: Swiss market quality premium (Roche, Novartis, Nestlé); CHF safe-haven adds multiple support

AVDE.name: Avantis International Equity ETF
AVDE.rationale: Developed markets value/small-cap tilt; discount to US multiple justified by lower growth

FENI.name: Fidelity Enhanced International ETF
FENI.rationale: Developed markets quant-active; similar profile to AVDE

SCHF.name: Schwab International Equity ETF
SCHF.rationale: Broad developed markets passive; slight premium over AVDE/FENI for breadth

VOO.name: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF
VOO.rationale: S&P 500 index ETF; switched to price-support methodology (index P/E approach produced T2 ceiling above ATH). Entry at 15–22% corrections from 52-wk high; T2 = 8% below ATH. No earnings multiple used.
VOO.eps_note: N/A — price-support methodology. T1 floor = 52-wk low ~$545; T1 ceil = 15% below ATH $699; T2 ceil = 8% below ATH $699.

HESM.name: Hess Midstream LP
HESM.rationale: MLP fee-based infrastructure; P/DCF on distributable cash flow ~$2.90/unit
HESM.eps_override_note: Use DCF/unit ~$2.90, not GAAP EPS. K-1 structure.

CEG.name: Constellation Energy
CEG.rationale: Nuclear fleet + AI data center PPAs; irreplaceable 24/7 carbon-free baseload

NEE.name: NextEra Energy
NEE.rationale: Regulated utility (FPL) + world's largest renewables developer; 28yr dividend growth

XLU.name: Utilities Select Sector SPDR ETF
XLU.rationale: US utilities sector ETF; defensive allocation + AI data center power theme

IAU.name: iShares Gold Trust
IAU.rationale: Physical gold ETF; price-based support levels. T1: $61-68 (52-wk low area), T2: $69-88
IAU.eps_override_note: No P/E. Use 52-week low and 200-day SMA as support levels for T1/T2.

REMX.name: VanEck Rare Earth/Strategic Metals ETF
REMX.rationale: Rare earth/strategic metals; hyper-cyclical. T1: $42-60 (near 52-wk low), T2: $61-88
REMX.eps_override_note: No P/E. Use historical support/resistance levels and 200-day SMA.

SOXQ.name: Invesco PHLX Semiconductor ETF
SOXQ.rationale: PHLX SOX semiconductor ETF; 0.19% ER vs. SOXX 0.35%. Full semi sector exposure.

BP.name: BP p.l.c.
BP.rationale: Integrated oil major; GAAP EPS distorted by impairments — use underlying FCF/earnings (~$3.50/ADR normalized). Cheapest oil major on EV/EBITDA (~3.9x). Activist Elliott pressure for capital return.
BP.eps_note: Use normalized underlying EPS ~$3.50 (not GAAP near-zero); FCF/share TTM ~$4.41 cross-check

SHEL.name: Shell plc
SHEL.rationale: Premium integrated oil major with world-class LNG franchise. Consistent EPS $5.06–$6.02 (2023–2025). CEO Wael Sawan executing disciplined FCF strategy. $3.5B/quarter buyback + growing dividend.
SHEL.eps_note: Use mid-cycle normalized EPS ~$6.00 (FY2025 actual: $6.02; 3yr avg: ~$5.59)
